---
name: Freeform Contribution (Advanced)
about: For contributors comfortable with markdown who prefer an unstructured format, or for contributions that don't fit the other templates.
title: ''

---

<!--
This is the freeform template for contributors who prefer working in markdown or whose contribution doesn't fit the structured forms.

If one of the guided templates (Challenge an Argument, Offer a Case Study, Share a Reference, Ask a Question, Flag a Missing Perspective) fits what you're doing, you may find those easier to work with — they're designed to help structure your thinking without requiring any technical knowledge.

This template is yours to shape however you like. The prompts below are suggestions, not requirements. Delete, rearrange or ignore them as needed.
-->

## What kind of contribution is this?

<!-- Optional. Helps with triage. Some possibilities: -->
<!-- Critique / Case Study / Reference / Question / Missing Perspective / Correction / Proposal / Something else entirely -->

## Which part of the framework does this relate to?

<!-- Optional. Link to specific documents if possible. -->
<!-- Core: Manifesto | Why Now | Why Not Already | Philosophical Foundation | Design Principles -->
<!-- Landscape: Top-Down & Bottom-Up | Where It Works | Where It Fails | Current Approaches | Case Studies -->
<!-- General: The whole approach / Multiple documents / Not sure -->

## Your contribution

<!-- Write freely. Plain language, academic language, bullet points, essay form, stream of consciousness — whatever works for you. -->

## References (if any)

<!-- Papers, books, URLs, people, organisations. Use whatever citation format you're comfortable with. -->

## Your background (optional)

<!-- What perspective are you coming from? Not required, but helpful context for understanding your contribution. -->

## Anything else?

<!-- Additional context, caveats, related thoughts, or questions for the author. -->
